title: 命案目睹记
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\136-命案目睹记-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Lucy explores the grounds of Laceford Manor, noting a well-kept vegetable patch and walking along a driveway that passes under an iron bridge near a railway track. She enters a narrow backstreet bordered by a factory wall and a railway embankment. At a local post office, Lucy uses a phone booth to call a woman, identifying herself as Miss Esperanza. She informs the woman that she has arrived safely and will report any new discoveries, requesting that the woman not disturb the recipient, who is currently resting.

Back at the manor, Lucy practices golf on the lawn. During a conversation, Mr. Crickenshaw expresses his refusal to die so that others can build houses on his land. He voices suspicions regarding Cedric, Harold, and Alfred, noting that his illness is mysterious and that even Dr. Campbell could not determine its cause.

The following day, while searching for a golf ball on the railway embankment, Lucy discovers broken thorny scrub, a piece of light brown fur caught on a thorn, and a cheap porcelain powder box among the weeds. She also observes blurred, old footprints in the grass. Lucy visits her aunt, Miss Marple, at Madison No. 4 to present the fur and the powder box. Miss Marple suggests the items fell from a woman in a light fur coat after her body was moved down the embankment. Miss Marple argues that this evidence supports a hypothesis of murder rather than a body being lost, suggesting the killer moved the body from the railway.
